jobs.name: task-queue-processor-runner
jobs.description: Minutely TQP runner for background task queue processing

payload.message:
Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-queue-processor.sh

Report exit code and any output. If nothing to process, output: TQP: idle

jobs.name: TQP Daily Runner (fixed)
jobs.description: TQP Daily Runner — renamed/fixed from disabled cron. Runs task-queue-processor.sh every 4 min silently.

payload.message: TQP_RUN: Run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-queue-processor.sh. This processes ONE task per run: picks up queued → dispatches, or verifies dispatched → marks done/re-queues/escalates. The script handles everything internally. No reply needed — script writes its own state. TKT-0501: if TQP escalation occurs, script uses sovereign-alert.sh (direct Bot API).

CONTEXT_EARLY_WARNING: Run 'bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/main-session-context-warning.sh' and report exit code. If exit=0, read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/main-session-context-ok.json and reply OK. If state/main-session-context-warning.json exists, alert Ken with its contents.

HEALTH_CHECK: Run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/health-check.sh silently. After run, read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/db-read.sh state_diagnostics — if consecutiveFailures >= 3 OR status=degraded, alert Ken via sovereign-alert.sh --source HEALTH. Otherwise output exactly: OK

TASK_MONITOR: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-collector.sh. Then use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/db-read.sh state_task_queue — check for stalled tasks. If non-empty, use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source TASK --message "<summary>". TKT-0501 fix: direct Bot API, no sessions_send.
MISSION_CONTROL_REFRESH: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/generate-mission-control.sh to regenerate the dashboard data. No reply needed.

TQP_RUN: Execute the task queue processor. Run:
bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-queue-processor.sh

This processes ONE task per run: picks up queued → dispatches, or verifies dispatched → marks done/re-queues/escalates. The script handles everything internally. Capture and report the exit code and key output lines. If escalation occurs (script calls sovereign-alert.sh), that happens independently.

No need to interpret or act on the output beyond reporting it briefly.

CONTEXT_WATCHDOG: Run 'bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/main-session-context-watchdog.sh' and report exit code. If exit=1 (warning) or exit=2 (critical), alert Ken with the state file contents. If exit=0, reply OK.

HEAP_CHECK: Run 'bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/gateway-heap-check.sh' and report exit code. If exit=1 (heap warning) or exit=2 (critical), alert Ken with the state file contents. If exit=0, reply OK.

AUTO-HEAL NIGHTLY: First load the pg-sprint-backlog skill: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/skill-load.sh pg-sprint-backlog. Then run zsh /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/auto-heal.sh. After completion, read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/auto-heal-current.json. For each needs_ken item, log it. Report summary: 'AUTO-HEAL: [N] checks run, [M] issues, [K] needs_ken items'. If needs_ken_count > 0, list the items with severity.

jobs.name: PG Sync Check — Hourly Stability Monitor
jobs.description: TKT-0268: DISABLED 2026-06-02 — PG is SSOT, JSON dual-write decommissioned. This cron was comparing PG against non-existent file. False alarm.

payload.message: Run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sync-check.sh. Compare output to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/cutover-baseline.txt. If any new mismatches appear (beyond known file-count differences), alert Ken via Telegram. This is TKT-0268 — 24h Postgres dual-write stability monitoring.
OBS_COLLECT: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/obs-collector.sh silently. No reply needed.

TZ_DRIFT_CHECK: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/tz-drift-monitor.sh. If exit code 2 (DRIFT_DETECTED), use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source TZDRIFT --file state/tz-drift-report.json. TKT-0501 fix. No reply if OK.

jobs.name: PG-Notion Batch Sync (hourly)
jobs.description: TKT-0406: Batch reconciliation — sync all tickets with notion_sync.status != 'synced' every 30 min

payload.message:
Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/pg-to-notion-sync.sh --batch

This syncs all tickets where notion_sync.status != 'synced'. It is self-healing — catches tickets whose event-driven sync failed. Process each ticket, apply rate limiting, update notion_sync metadata on success.

If errors: log to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/pg-notion-sync-errors.json
If 3 consecutive failures for same ticket: log to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/pg-notion-sync-dlq.json

payload.message: WARDEN: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/warden-cron.sh. If drift detected (state/warden-escalation-pending.json created), use the exec tool to forward to Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh (TKT-0501 fix: direct Bot API, bypasses session-lane hijack). No reply if OK.

DOD_VALIDATE: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/dod-validator.sh. If exit non-zero or state/dod-validation-alert.json created, use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source DOD. TKT-0501 fix. No reply if all clear.

jobs.name: Nightly Gateway Restart (03:00 AEST — systemEvent)
jobs.description: Nightly Gateway Restart (03:00 AEST) — writes marker then restarts. Cron will report 'interrupted' — expected. Verification by 03:05 follow-up cron.

payload.text: NIGHTLY_RESTART: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/nightly-gateway-restart.sh. This session WILL BE INTERRUPTED by gateway restart — expected. Follow-up verification at 03:05.

jobs.name: Nightly Gateway Restart Verify (03:05 AEST — systemEvent)
jobs.description: Post-restart verification (03:05 AEST) — checks marker from 03:00 restart cron, verifies gateway is alive, sends success/failure to Ken via Telegram. Part of CHG-0411 two-cron design.

payload.text: NIGHTLY_RESTART_VERIFY: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/nightly-restart-verify.sh. If output is success/failure: use the exec tool to forward to Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source RESTART. TKT-0501 fix. If 'no restart marker found': silent.

payload.message: STALE_CLEANUP: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/stale-task-cleanup.sh. Clean temp files > 48h in tmp/ and state/cache/. If items cleaned, use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source STALE_CLEANUP --message "STALE_CLEANUP:[count] items removed".

MODEL_CONTEXT_SYNC: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sync-model-context.sh && bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/apply-model-context.sh. No reply needed.

AKB_HOLOCRON: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/akb-sync.sh. If changes found: use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source AKB_HOLOCRON --message "AKB_HOLOCRON: Changes detected". If no changes: silent. Update akb-sync-state.json.

TRIGGER-04/06: Monitor OpenClaw upstream version and auto-update rules.

1. Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/version-check.sh
2. Check state/critical-config-baseline.json for config drift
3. If version drift detected: invoke 'sovereign-alert.sh --source TRIGGER-04 --message TRIGGER-04-CHANGED:[current]→[available]' to send Telegram to Ken (8574109706).
4. If config drift detected: invoke 'sovereign-alert.sh --source TRIGGER-06 --message TRIGGER-06: Config drift detected — [fields] changed from baseline'
5. If clean: output exactly 'OK: no drift'

Always output exactly one line.

MEMORY_HYGIENE: Clean up old state files and archived memory.

1. Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/memory-hygiene.sh
2. Remove memory archive files older than 90 days from state/memory-archive/
3. Remove old cost-state backups older than 30 days
4. If cleaned > 0: output "HYGIENE: [N] files archived, [M] removed"
5. If no action: output "HYGIENE: no cleanup needed"

Always output exactly one line.

Use the exec tool to run this shell command: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/generate-standup.sh

The script reads state files and builds the 8-section stand-up HTML, piping it to the Canvas document at ~/.openclaw/canvas/documents/standup-daily/index.html. Do NOT use the write tool — the script handles file output via its own pipe. No reply needed unless the script exits non-zero. If idempotency blocks a re-run (date already generated), set STANDUP_FORCE=1 in the environment before invoking.

Operational run of the Ollama dashboard usage scraper (TKT-0533).

Run this command with exec using absolute paths only:
  bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/ollama-usage-scraper-run.sh

It starts the OpenClaw browser, scrapes ollama.com/settings, updates cost-state.json, then stops the browser. Do not modify the script. Report exit code and the scraper output summary line.

BACKUP_DIRECT: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/backup.sh. If exit non-zero, use the exec tool to log incident via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/incident-log.sh. Do NOT write or overwrite state/backup-state.json — that file is owned by scripts/backup.sh. No reply needed if OK.

MIDDAY_COST: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/cost-tracker.sh silently. After run, use the exec tool to read: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/db-read.sh state_cost. No reply needed.

Run canonical session maintenance per CHG-0831. Execute: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/run-openclaw-sessions-cleanup.sh --enforce. Uses OpenClaw session.maintenance config + openclaw sessions cleanup --all-agents --enforce. Log results and report summary to Yoda/main session.

SHIELD_SECURITY_SWEEP: Daily security review — Shield Rule 1 enforcement.

1. Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/shield-qa-pending.json (if exists)
2. For each pending asset: run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/shield-check.sh with its metadata
3. For each item, also perform LLM-level review: read the asset content, check S1-S5 with reasoning, flag anything the script may have missed
4. Write final verdict to shield-qa-log.json
5. If FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/shield-escalation-pending.json
6. If all clear or no pending items: output exactly 'SHIELD: clear' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

LEX_LEGAL_SWEEP: Daily legal review — Lex Rule 1 enforcement.

1. Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/lex-qa-pending.json (if exists)
2. For each pending asset: run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/lex-check.sh with its metadata
3. For each item, also perform LLM-level legal review: read the asset content and original brief, apply L1-L5 checks with full legal reasoning, flag contractual risk, regulatory issues, liability, IP, missing disclosures. Note: Lex flags risk — not a substitute for qualified legal advice on matters >A$10,000
4. Write final verdict to lex-qa-log.json
5. If FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/lex-escalation-pending.json
6. If all clear or no pending items: output exactly 'LEX: clear' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

SAGE_QA_SWEEP: Daily QA review — Sage Rule 1 enforcement.

## Step 1 — Check pending queue
Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/sage-qa-pending.json (if exists).
For each pending asset: read the asset file, run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sage-qa.sh with its metadata, write verdict to sage-qa-log.json, if FAIL write to sage-qa-failures.json, mark PASS as cleared.

## Step 2 — LLM checks
For each item: check (1) meets the brief? (2) clear conclusion/CTA? (3) facts verifiable? Cross-check numbers against state files.

## Step 3 — Escalate failures
If any FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/sage-escalation-pending.json

## Step 4 — Output
If no pending items or all PASS: output exactly 'SAGE: clear' and stop.
If escalated: output exactly 'SAGE: escalated [N] items' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

payload.text:
ARIA_CONTEXT_SYNC: Update the shared knowledge bridge for Aria before daily close.

## 0. Pre-flight: Delegated Auth Check (TKT-0336)
Use the exec tool to run: zsh /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/check-delegated-auth.sh --json
Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/delegated-auth-status.json
If allValid=false: include a ⚠️ AUTH ALERT section in yoda-daily-brief.md listing which accounts need re-auth and the exact gog command to fix them.

## 1. Read today's work
- memory/2026-MM-DD.md and memory/CHANGELOG.md (today's CHG entries)

## 2. Read shared decisions
- memory/shared/decisions.md (today's decisions)

## 3. Write/update yoda-daily-brief.md

## 4. Review training-pipeline.md

## 5. Git commit
Use the exec tool to run: bash -c 'cd /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git commit -m "chore: yoda daily context sync YYYY-MM-DD"'

Keep language accessible — Aria and Angie will read this, not just Ken.

payload.text:

If /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/eod-blocked-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).json exists, exit 0. Do not proceed.

No reply if all passed.

payload.text: WEEKLY_RESTART: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/nightly-gateway-restart.sh. Same script as nightly. This session WILL BE INTERRUPTED — expected.

jobs.name: AInchors Weekly Asset Review
jobs.description: Weekly asset registry review — flags stale assets, updates Notion, keeps AInchors assets evergreen.

payload.message:
Answer directly. No reasoning. No explanation. Output only the answer.

ASSET_REVIEW: Run the weekly asset review. Execute: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/asset-review.sh — review the output. For each asset flagged as Needs Review: read the file, read recent decisions from memory/shared/decisions.md, update the asset to reflect current state. Update Notion Asset Registry with new Last Updated dates and Status. Report summary of what was reviewed and updated.

Run Aria's weekly business ROI report. Execute exactly:

bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace-business/scripts/business-roi-report.sh

This reads state/business-roi.json and state/business-value-rubric.json, aggregates the current ISO week's entries, and sends Angie a Telegram summary via Aria's bot token. Do not modify any state files. Report success or any error verbatim.

payload.text:
MONTHLY_SLA_REPORT: Generate SLA report for the previous month.

Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sla-report-cron.sh

The wrapper computes the previous month in AEST, runs scripts/sla-report.sh YYYY-MM, and writes reports/sla-YYYY-MM.md plus appends to memory/shared/sla-history.md.

No agentTurn. No native write tool. No reply needed unless exit non-zero.
